items.title: Muxer: Open-source model multiplexer for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This tool routes heavy tasks to Claude and typing or boilerplate to cheaper models (like DeepSeek) right inside Claude Code, printing your exact savings. Since you use Claude Code daily and want to optimize your workflow, this directly cuts your API bill without losing quality. Try installing it today and running your next Claude Code session through it to see how much you save while building AI-ingest.

items.title: Decispher: Share context between human devs and AI agents
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer just shipped this SaaS to help humans and AI agents stay in sync using features like 'Branch Story' (explaining git branches) and an MCP tool for context transfer. It is a real, shipped product built for the exact agent-led workflow you use. Try signing up for their free tier this week to transfer context between your Claude Code terminal and your browser chat when debugging your Supabase database.

items.title: ctx: Search your local coding agent history
items.why_it_matters: Coding agents don't have long-term memory, but they leave full transcripts on your machine. This Rust CLI indexes those local logs into a structured SQLite database so you can search them instantly. You can run this tool today to search your past Claude Code sessions and quickly retrieve the exact Supabase setup commands or Next.js configurations you used in your first project.

items.title: Piggy: 'Lazy senior dev' mode for AI agents
items.why_it_matters: This tool forces AI agents to write 80-94% less code by focusing only on what actually needs to change, preventing them from rewriting entire files. This keeps your git diffs clean and easy to understand as you learn version control. Try running your next Next.js feature prompt through Piggy to get clean, targeted code edits instead of massive, messy file rewrites.

items.title: Flashtype: Markdown editor for Claude and Codex with in-line diffs
items.why_it_matters: A local markdown editor designed for collaborating with Claude and Codex that features inline diffs so you can quickly accept or reject changes. This is perfect for managing your project's markdown files, system prompts, or your profile.md. Try opening your AI-ingest profile in Flashtype this week to visually track and merge the changes Claude suggests.
